advantages.notes: Marty found the Espada hidden immunity idol on Day 6 with help from his ally Jill Behm. After the tribe swap on Day 12, Marty's position became precarious. In Episode 6, Sash Lenahan blackmailed Marty into giving up the idol in exchange for safety, promising to return it if they lost another immunity challenge. Sash never returned it. Marty was voted out on Day 24 as the 10th person eliminated.

Sash obtained Marty's idol through a deal where he promised to return it but never did. He played it on himself at the Final 5 tribal council on Day 36 (Episode 14, 'This Is Going to Hurt'), negating 1 vote against him from Jane. Jane Bright was voted out unanimously that night.

NaOnka wrestled Kelly B. for a clue to the Hidden Immunity Idol during a reward challenge on Day 8. She and Brenda Lowe then found the La Flor idol together at camp. NaOnka never played the idol herself. On Day 28, she gave the idol to Chase Rice before quitting the game. She became the first contestant to find a Hidden Immunity Idol before quitting Survivor.

Chase received NaOnka's idol on Day 28 when she decided to quit the game. He played it on himself at the Final 5 tribal council on Day 36 (Episode 14, 'This Is Going to Hurt'), but no votes were cast against him, making it an unsuccessful play. Jane Bright was voted out that night.

A unique advantage exclusive to Season 21. The Medallion of Power gave a tribe a head start in challenges. The younger La Flor tribe found it first but traded it to Espada in exchange for flint and fishing gear. It was used in several immunity challenges before being retired at the tribe swap on Day 12. It was not an idol and could not be played at tribal council.
